mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth remains positive.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133755.0 thousand as of Oct 2012 shows continued employment gains
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with stable labor demand
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.04 over 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% (effective zero lower bound), the Fed signals commitment to monetary accommodation. This threshold-triggered signal activates only when FEDFUNDS is constrained at the ZLB, predicting positive equity returns as investors rotate into risk assets seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of Oct 2012 confirms ZLB regime
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: During ZLB periods (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%), 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return ≤ 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or signal produces negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with increasing retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer and construction demand, signaling economic acceleration. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both series to show month-over-month growth, predicting positive equity returns as forward earnings expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both show positive month-over-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 872.0 thousand and RSAFS at 411591.0 million as of Sep-Oct 2012
regime_hint: demand-led recovery phase with synchronized housing and consumption growth
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints, reducing near-term earnings growth expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth forecasts downward.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM but 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk, triggering a flight-to-safety rotation that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp over 3 months but 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances, or mean forward return during deterioration periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, a monetary policy lag emerges. This regime-conditional interaction predicts negative equity returns over 20 days as markets anticipate future Fed tightening and real rate compression.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 231.751 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of Oct 2012
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Trigger condition met but 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration when comparing the latest quarter to the prior-year quarter. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as sustained economic expansion supports corporate profit growth and equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13616.2 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2012
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth trajectory
failure_condition: Positive YoY GDP growth fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) both declining month-over-month signals synchronized supply and labor weakness, indicating recessionary pressure. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.0328 and PAYEMS at 133755.0 as of Sep-Oct 2012
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor contraction or early recession
failure_condition: Joint MoM declines in INDPRO and PAYEMS fail to produce negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction < 0.04 over any 2-year window.
